workload_profile.reasoning: EKS-based platform for backend and security workloads, RDS PostgreSQL, ECR, Secrets Manager, KMS. Variable-driven configuration with per-environment tfvars (dev, stage, prod). Multi-AZ optional. VPC endpoints for cost optimization. Security-by-default (private EKS endpoint, optional CloudTrail/KMS). Supports taskforge-backend and taskforge-security.

security.rationale: IRSA for ESO and LB controller; RDS in private subnets; storage_encrypted; VPC Flow Logs; optional KMS and CloudTrail. ECR scan_on_push, image_tag_mutability IMMUTABLE. Per-environment tfvars with ADD_VALUE_HERE and REPLACE_WITH_SECURE_PASSWORD placeholders. db_password in state remains a gap.
evidence_found: IRSA for External Secrets Operator and AWS Load Balancer Controller (iam.tf)
RDS in private subnets, publicly_accessible=false (rds.tf)
RDS storage_encrypted=true; optional customer KMS (kms.tf, rds.tf)
VPC Flow Logs to CloudWatch (vpc.tf)
ECR scan_on_push, image_tag_mutability=IMMUTABLE (ecr.tf)
Secrets Manager with optional KMS (secrets.tf)
CloudTrail S3 bucket encryption, versioning, public access block (cloudtrail.tf)
ESO IAM policy scoped to workload secret ARNs (iam.tf)
ECR push policy scoped to repository ARNs; GetAuthorizationToken * per AWS docs (iam.tf)
Per-environment tfvars templates: dev.tfvars.example, stage.tfvars.example, prod.tfvars.example with placeholders
missing_evidence: Secrets rotation or mutation strategy documented
db_password flows through Terraform state via secrets.tf
key_risks: Placeholder secrets (REPLACE_WITH_*) must be replaced before production
RDS password and secret values in Terraform state
recommended_actions: Use external secret creation or bootstrap for DB password to avoid state
Enable use_customer_managed_kms and enable_cloudtrail for compliance

reliability.rationale: VPC with 2 AZs, public and private subnets. RDS Multi-AZ optional. EKS node group in private subnets across AZs. Backup retention and final snapshot configurable via variables.
evidence_found: VPC with vpc_az_count (default 2), public and private subnets (vpc.tf)
RDS db_subnet_group in private subnets (rds.tf)
rds_multi_az variable (default false; set true for prod)
rds_backup_retention_period, rds_backup_window, rds_skip_final_snapshot configurable (variables.tf)
EKS node group subnet_ids = private subnets (eks.tf)
NAT gateway optional; VPC endpoints reduce NAT dependency (vpc.tf, vpc_endpoints.tf)
key_risks: rds_backup_retention_period default 0; rds_skip_final_snapshot default true — prod must override
recommended_actions: Set rds_backup_retention_period=7 and rds_skip_final_snapshot=false for prod

performance_efficiency.rationale: VPC interface endpoints (ECR, Logs) and S3 gateway endpoint reduce NAT data transfer. EKS in private subnets. Instance types configurable.
evidence_found: S3 gateway endpoint always enabled (vpc_endpoints.tf)
ECR API and DKR interface endpoints when enable_vpc_endpoints=true (vpc_endpoints.tf)
CloudWatch Logs interface endpoint (vpc_endpoints.tf)
eks_node_instance_types, rds_instance_class configurable (variables.tf)
enable_nat_gateway=false option when VPC endpoints suffice (variables.tf)

cost_optimization.rationale: Variable-driven cost controls: NAT optional, CloudTrail optional, observability alarms optional, customer KMS optional. Cost allocation tags on all resources. ECR lifecycle policy. Per-environment tfvars support cost-minimization defaults.
evidence_found: default_tags with Project, Environment, CostCenter, Owner (main.tf)
enable_nat_gateway, nat_gateway_count configurable (variables.tf)
enable_cloudtrail, enable_observability_alarms, use_customer_managed_kms optional (variables.tf)
ECR lifecycle policy keep last N images (ecr.tf)
VPC endpoints reduce NAT data transfer cost (vpc_endpoints.tf)
Per-environment tfvars for dev/stage/prod cost profiles

operational_excellence.rationale: Terraform CI: validate (fmt, init, validate) and plan. Bootstrap script for backend. IAM execution policy documented. Manual apply is approval gate. Per-environment tfvars improve configuration clarity.
evidence_found: GitHub Actions and GitLab CI: validate → plan on terraform/** changes
bootstrap-backend.sh for S3 + DynamoDB state backend
iam-execution-policy.json and docs/iam-execution-requirements.md
terraform.tfvars.example, dev.tfvars.example, stage.tfvars.example, prod.tfvars.example
backend.hcl.example
README with Quick Start, Post-Apply, Configuration Model
missing_evidence: CI uses cp backend.hcl.example backend.hcl — may fail if backend not bootstrapped
key_risks: Plan job copies backend.hcl.example to backend.hcl; backend must exist
recommended_actions: Consider OIDC for CI AWS credentials instead of long-lived keys

observability.rationale: VPC Flow Logs, RDS CloudWatch logs, optional CloudTrail. CloudWatch alarms for EKS 5xx and RDS CPU/connections exist but disabled by default (enable_observability_alarms=false).
evidence_found: VPC Flow Logs to CloudWatch (vpc.tf)
RDS enabled_cloudwatch_logs_exports = postgresql (rds.tf)
CloudWatch alarms for EKS control plane 5xx, RDS CPU, RDS connections (observability.tf)
Alarm thresholds and evaluation periods configurable (variables.tf)
missing_evidence: enable_observability_alarms default false — alarms not created in dev
No SNS topic or alarm action for notifications
key_risks: Alarms disabled by default; prod must set enable_observability_alarms=true
recommended_actions: Add alarm action (SNS topic) for critical alarms

compliance_evidence_quality.rationale: CloudTrail optional with management events, log validation. VPC Flow Logs. RDS encryption. No explicit compliance mapping document.
evidence_found: CloudTrail with enable_log_file_validation, include_global_service_events (cloudtrail.tf)
VPC Flow Logs (vpc.tf)
RDS storage encryption, CloudWatch logs (rds.tf)
data_classification, lifecycle_stage tags (variables.tf)
missing_evidence: Compliance control mapping (NIST, etc.) not documented

missing_components.reason: Placeholder secrets (REPLACE_WITH_*, ADD_VALUE_HERE) in tfvars templates; must be replaced before production apply

production_baseline.conditional_reason: CONDITIONAL: Placeholder secrets must be replaced before production. db_password flows through Terraform state. Prod tfvars should set rds_backup_retention_period, rds_skip_final_snapshot=false, rds_multi_az=true, enable_observability_alarms=true, and optionally enable_cloudtrail and use_customer_managed_kms.

findings.title: Placeholder secrets in tfvars templates
findings.summary: dev.tfvars.example, stage.tfvars.example, prod.tfvars.example contain ADD_VALUE_HERE and REPLACE_WITH_SECURE_PASSWORD. secrets_workload_placeholders default has REPLACE_WITH_OPENSSL_RAND_HEX_32_OUTPUT and REPLACE_WITH_STRONG_API_KEY.
findings.evidence: dev.tfvars.example: project=ADD_VALUE_HERE, db_password=REPLACE_WITH_SECURE_PASSWORD. variables.tf lines 268-275: secrets_workload_placeholders default = { backend = { secret_key = REPLACE_WITH_OPENSSL_RAND_HEX_32_OUTPUT }, security = { api_key = REPLACE_WITH_STRONG_API_KEY } }.
findings.impact: Production deployment with unmodified placeholders will use insecure values; JWT signing and API key auth will be weak or predictable.
findings.recommendation: Replace all placeholders before prod: generate SECRET_KEY (openssl rand -hex 32) and API_KEY; store via TF_VAR, -var at apply, or Vault. Document in README that placeholders must never be used in production.

steps: Set secrets_workload_placeholders in prod.tfvars with real values, or use separate bootstrap to populate Secrets Manager
Never commit real secrets; use TF_VAR or -var at apply
validation_steps: Verify no ADD_VALUE_HERE or REPLACE_WITH in prod tfvars; verify Secrets Manager has real values

findings.title: RDS password in Terraform state
findings.summary: db_password flows through secrets.tf into Terraform state; terraform-deployment-checklist requires secrets outside state.
findings.evidence: secrets.tf line 21 embeds var.db_password in secret_string for workloads with db_url. rds.tf line 38 uses var.db_password. Both flow through Terraform state.
findings.impact: Terraform state contains sensitive credentials; state compromise exposes DB password. Compliance risk for regulated environments.
findings.recommendation: Use external secret creation: bootstrap script or separate pipeline creates RDS and Secrets Manager secret without storing password in state. Or use SSM Parameter Store with data source; avoid passing password through Terraform.

steps: Document: db_password must be set via TF_VAR or -var at apply; never commit
For prod: use AWS Secrets Manager rotation or bootstrap that creates secret outside Terraform
Consider RDS IAM auth for app to reduce need for static password
validation_steps: Ensure remote state with encryption; audit state access

findings.title: RDS backup and final snapshot defaults unsafe for production
findings.evidence: variables.tf: rds_backup_retention_period default = 0, rds_skip_final_snapshot default = true. prod.tfvars.example does not override these.
findings.impact: No automated backups; destroy leaves no final snapshot. Data loss risk on failure or accidental destroy.
findings.recommendation: Add prod-specific overrides to prod.tfvars.example: rds_backup_retention_period = 7, rds_skip_final_snapshot = false, rds_multi_az = true.

steps: Add to prod.tfvars.example: rds_backup_retention_period = 7, rds_skip_final_snapshot = false, rds_multi_az = true
validation_steps: terraform plan -var-file=prod.tfvars; verify backup settings in AWS

findings.title: Observability alarms disabled by default; prod.tfvars.example lacks override
findings.summary: enable_observability_alarms default false; EKS and RDS alarms exist in code but are not created. prod.tfvars.example does not set enable_observability_alarms=true.
findings.evidence: variables.tf line 283: enable_observability_alarms default = false. observability.tf uses var.enable_observability_alarms to gate alarm creation.
findings.impact: EKS control plane 5xx and RDS CPU/connections alarms not created; issues may go undetected in production.
findings.recommendation: Add enable_observability_alarms = true to prod.tfvars.example. Consider adding alarm action (SNS topic) for critical alarms.

steps: Optionally add aws_sns_topic and alarm action for notifications
validation_steps: terraform plan; verify alarms in CloudWatch

findings.title: CloudTrail and customer KMS disabled by default
findings.summary: enable_cloudtrail default false; use_customer_managed_kms default false. API audit and key control not enabled.
findings.evidence: variables.tf: enable_cloudtrail default = false, use_customer_managed_kms default = false.
findings.impact: No API audit trail; AWS-managed keys used. Compliance and forensics gaps for regulated environments.
findings.recommendation: Add enable_cloudtrail = true and use_customer_managed_kms = true to prod.tfvars.example for compliance.

steps: Add enable_cloudtrail = true, use_customer_managed_kms = true to prod.tfvars.example
validation_steps: terraform plan; verify trail and KMS keys in AWS

findings.title: CI uses long-lived AWS credentials
findings.summary: GitHub Actions and GitLab CI use AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID and AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY.
findings.evidence: terraform.yml lines 62-66: aws-access-key-id and aws-secret-access-key from secrets. GitLab CI variables comment references same.
findings.impact: Long-lived credentials increase blast radius if leaked; rotation burden.
findings.recommendation: Use OIDC with aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials role-to-assume for GitHub; GitLab OIDC for GitLab CI.

steps: Create IAM role with OIDC trust for GitHub/GitLab
Remove AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID and AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY from secrets

remediation_summary.ordering_reasoning: F1 (placeholder secrets) is security_blocker — must fix before prod per docs/remediation-ordering.md. F2 (secrets in state) is security improvement. F3 (RDS backup) is reliability. F4–F6 are observability, compliance, and ops improvements.

evidence_found: VPC with public/private subnets, 2 AZs, NAT optional, Flow Logs
EKS in private subnets, IRSA for ESO and LB controller
RDS PostgreSQL in private subnets, encrypted, optional Multi-AZ
ECR with scan_on_push, lifecycle policy, IMMUTABLE tags
Secrets Manager with workload-specific secrets, optional KMS
KMS keys for RDS and Secrets Manager when use_customer_managed_kms=true
CloudTrail with random_id bucket, encryption, versioning when enabled
VPC endpoints: S3 gateway, ECR API/DKR, Logs interface
Cost allocation tags (Project, Environment, CostCenter, Owner)
Terraform CI: validate, plan on GitHub Actions and GitLab CI
IAM execution policy and bootstrap script
Per-environment tfvars: dev.tfvars.example, stage.tfvars.example, prod.tfvars.example
Variable-driven configuration; all customization via .tfvars
CloudTrail depends_on bucket policy (terraform-apply-order compliant)
missing_evidence: Secrets rotation or external mutation strategy

diagram.content:
flowchart TB
  subgraph Internet["Internet"]
    Users[Users]
  end

  subgraph VPC["VPC (10.0.0.0/16)"]
    subgraph Public["Public Subnets"]
      IGW[Internet Gateway]
      NAT[NAT Gateway optional]
    end
    subgraph Private["Private Subnets (2 AZs)"]
      EKS[EKS Cluster]
      subgraph Workloads["Workloads"]
        Backend[taskforge-backend]
        Security[taskforge-security]
      end
    end
    subgraph Data["Data Layer"]
      RDS[(RDS PostgreSQL)]
    end
  end

  subgraph AWS_Services["AWS Services"]
    ECR[ECR Repos]
    SM[Secrets Manager]
    KMS[KMS optional]
  end

  subgraph Observability["Observability"]
    VFL[VPC Flow Logs]
    CT[CloudTrail optional]
    CW[CloudWatch Alarms optional]
  end

  Users --> IGW
  IGW --> Public
  EKS -->|IRSA/ESO| SM
  EKS -->|Pull| ECR
  Backend --> RDS
  Security --> SM
  RDS -->|Encrypted| KMS
  SM -->|Encrypted| KMS
  VPC --> VFL
  VPC --> CT
diagram.caption: TaskForge platform architecture: EKS in private subnets, RDS, ECR, Secrets Manager; VPC endpoints; optional NAT, CloudTrail, KMS.

deployment_frequency.evidence: Terraform CI triggers on push to main/master when terraform/** changes. Plan runs; apply is manual.
deployment_frequency.recommendation: Track actual apply frequency; add deployment metrics if automated apply is introduced.

lead_time.evidence: Pipeline: validate (fmt, init, validate) → plan. Manual apply after plan.
lead_time.recommendation: Track commit-to-apply time if automation increases.

change_failure_rate.evidence: No runtime or incident data in repository.
change_failure_rate.recommendation: Track Terraform apply failures; integrate with incident system.

mttr.evidence: No incident or runbook data in repository.
mttr.recommendation: Add runbook linkage; integrate with PagerDuty/Opsgenie for alert-to-resolution.
